predictions.text: Canada’s Bill C-22 (lawful access / metadata surveillance bill) will receive Royal Assent and become law.
predictions.verification_criteria: Parl.gc.ca bill status (or Canada Gazette) shows Bill C-22 has received Royal Assent on or before the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON notes Bill C-22 would mandate mass metadata surveillance and is active in Canadian policy debate.
Canadian security/telecom policy bills often advance during heightened security narratives.
monitoring_signals: House of Commons second/third reading votes scheduled or completed
Senate committee hearings and amendments reported
Government news releases prioritizing 'lawful access' legislation
predictions.rationale: The bill is already salient and framed as a security necessity; legislative momentum is plausible within 2026, but Senate scrutiny and civil-liberties resistance reduce odds of enactment by year-end.

predictions.text: DHS will expand the H-2A eligible-country list by adding at least 5 countries relative to the most recent prior designation list.
predictions.verification_criteria: A DHS Federal Register notice (or DHS press release) updating H-2A eligible countries shows net additions of ≥5 countries versus the immediately preceding official list.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON highlights U.S. reliance on migrant labor programs to address farm-worker shortages.
Eligible-country lists are periodically updated via formal notices.
monitoring_signals: DHS/State Department consultations on seasonal worker programs
Farm-state lobbying and USDA statements on labor gaps
Draft or proposed Federal Register notices related to H-2 programs
predictions.rationale: Labor shortages increase incentives to broaden recruitment channels, and list updates are an administratively straightforward lever compared with major statutory reform.

predictions.text: Cuba will experience a nationwide disruption of internet access lasting at least 12 consecutive hours, as measured by a major internet-monitoring group.
predictions.verification_criteria: NetBlocks (or Access Now / IODA) reports a nationwide Cuba internet shutdown/disruption persisting ≥12 consecutive hours on or before the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON describes deepening hardship and unusual public defiance in Cuba, increasing regime-control incentives.
monitoring_signals: Reports of large protests, strikes, or unrest in multiple provinces
State media messaging about 'security' and 'public order'
Real-time connectivity anomalies flagged by NetBlocks/IODA
predictions.rationale: Cuba has precedent for restricting connectivity during unrest; worsening economic stress raises shutdown risk, but the economic and diplomatic costs keep probability moderate.

predictions.text: At least one reputable French polling firm will show a National Rally (RN) or RN-aligned candidate in first place for first-round voting intention in the 2026 Marseille mayoral race.
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly released poll by IFOP, Ipsos, Harris Interactive, Elabe, or OpinionWay reports RN (or explicit RN-allied slate) leading first-round vote intention for Marseille mayor by percentage points (ranked #1) on or before the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON frames the Marseille mayoral race as a bellwether for France’s trajectory including far-right strength.
monitoring_signals: National RN polling momentum and local candidate announcements
Polling releases specifically for Marseille municipal election
Coalition/endorsement shifts among center-right local lists
predictions.rationale: If the race is increasingly nationalized, RN’s chance to top a first-round poll rises; however, municipal dynamics and fragmented slates make sustained #1 polling uncertain.

predictions.text: CDC (or ACIP documentation) will announce a new or expanded active surveillance effort for COVID vaccine adverse events that explicitly states coverage of at least 5 million vaccinated individuals via linked EHR/claims/registry data.
predictions.verification_criteria: CDC/ACIP meeting materials, MMWR, or an official CDC press release describes an active surveillance program/pilot for COVID vaccine safety with stated population coverage ≥5,000,000 individuals using linked healthcare data.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON notes a report urging sweeping changes to detect and monitor Covid vaccine harms, increasing institutional pressure for upgrades.
monitoring_signals: ACIP agenda items on post-market surveillance reform
HHS/CDC procurement or funding notices for surveillance analytics
MMWR publications describing new safety-monitoring methods
predictions.rationale: Public trust and policy pressure can drive measurable surveillance expansions, but implementation timelines, privacy constraints, and inter-agency data-linking complexity reduce near-term likelihood.

predictions.text: A 2026 quarterly U.S. ‘Names of Persons Who Have Chosen to Expatriate’ Federal Register list will contain at least 3,500 names.
predictions.verification_criteria: Any quarterly Federal Register publication in 2026 titled ‘Names of Persons Who Have Chosen to Expatriate’ lists ≥3,500 individuals (counting names) by the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON reports the State Department cut the citizenship renunciation fee to $450, potentially lowering barriers.
monitoring_signals: Federal Register quarterly expatriation lists and counts
Tax/expatriation advisory firm commentary on demand surge
Consular appointment backlogs for renunciation services
predictions.rationale: Lower fees and administrative accessibility can lift the tail of renunciation volumes, but the process remains burdensome and driven by broader tax/immigration factors.

predictions.text: China will announce a nationwide reduction setting the minimum down payment ratio for first-home mortgages to 15% or lower.
predictions.verification_criteria: A PBoC, State Council, or national financial regulator announcement (or authoritative Xinhua/People’s Daily report of such a policy) states a nationwide first-home minimum down payment ratio ≤15% by the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON notes China data beating forecasts and easing property contraction, consistent with incremental stabilization policy.
monitoring_signals: PBoC/NRFC statements on housing support
Major-city mortgage policy changes later standardized nationally
Developer funding/support announcements linked to homebuyer demand
predictions.rationale: Authorities have strong incentives to support housing demand and confidence; lowering down payments is a high-impact, administratively direct tool, though policymakers may prefer more targeted local measures.

predictions.text: At some point, CME FedWatch (or an equivalent widely used futures-implied tool) will show at least a 50% probability of a Fed rate hike at the next scheduled FOMC meeting.
predictions.verification_criteria: A timestamped capture from CME FedWatch (or Refinitiv/Bloomberg equivalent probabilities derived from fed funds futures) shows P(hike at next meeting) ≥ 50% on any day on or before the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON notes growing chatter that the Fed’s next move could be a hike amid sticky inflation risk.
monitoring_signals: Core inflation surprises and near-term inflation expectations
Fedspeak emphasizing upside inflation risks
Front-end rates repricing (SOFR/fed funds futures) after CPI/PCE prints
predictions.rationale: Even without an actual hike, market-implied odds can swing quickly under energy/inflation shocks; however, the hurdle to reach ≥50% for the next meeting remains substantial.

predictions.text: FAO’s global Food Price Index will print at least 10% year-over-year higher in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: FAO Food Price Index (headline) YoY change is ≥ +10.0% in any monthly FAO release dated on or before the deadline.
evidence: STRUCTURED_SUMMARY_JSON emphasizes elevated oil prices from the Iran conflict, which can feed through to fertilizer, freight, and food costs.
monitoring_signals: Crude/freight cost persistence and fertilizer price indices
Wheat/corn/rice futures and export restrictions
FAO sub-index trends (cereals, oils, sugar) accelerating upward
predictions.rationale: Energy and shipping disruptions can amplify food inflation quickly, but harvest outcomes and policy interventions (stock releases/export policies) could cap the YoY rise.

predictions.text: At least one of WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, iMessage, or Meta Messenger will publicly ship post-quantum cryptography (PQC) for end-to-end encryption in a production release (not a research blog only).
predictions.verification_criteria: Official release notes, security documentation, or a vendor blog post tied to a production rollout states that PQC (e.g., ML-KEM/Kyber or other NIST PQC) is deployed for E2EE key establishment/handshake for at least some users by the deadline.
evidence: Broader industry migration toward PQC is accelerating as NIST standards mature and large platforms harden E2EE against long-term threats.
monitoring_signals: Client release notes mentioning PQC/ML-KEM/Kyber
Open-source code commits enabling PQC in protocol libraries
Third-party cryptography audits referencing PQC deployment
predictions.rationale: Large messaging platforms are among the most motivated to adopt PQC for E2EE at scale; timelines are plausible within 2026, though rollout risk and compatibility constraints can delay broad deployment.
